evals.prompt: Install SpecKit in this empty repository for both Claude and Codex integrations.
evals.expected_output: Bootstraps the SpecKit CLI if missing, detects no existing .specify/ directory, asks (or honors) the dual-integration choice, runs specify init for the first integration and specify integration install for the second, and reports the install summary with a restart reminder.
expectations: Checks for the specify CLI with a path-normalized `command -v specify` probe before mutating anything, including `$HOME/.local/bin` for uv tool installs
If the CLI is missing, recommends `uv tool install specify-cli --from git+https://github.com/github/spec-kit.git`
Detects .specify/ presence and confirms it is ABSENT before proceeding
Runs `specify init --here --integration claude` (or similar) as the bootstrap call
Runs `specify integration install codex` (or `--integration-options="--skills"`) for the second integration
Reports the installed integrations and their artifact paths
Tells the user to restart the coding-agent process so new skills load

evals.prompt: Install SpecKit in this repo — but I already have .specify/ from a previous install.
evals.expected_output: Detects the existing install, refuses to overwrite, and hands off to $speckit-upgrade (or offers to add a new integration alongside the existing ones via specify integration install).
expectations: Runs `specify integration list` to show what is already installed
States that the right tool for an existing install is $speckit-upgrade, not $speckit-install
Does NOT run `specify init --here --force` (force is reserved for the upgrade skill with backup/restore)
Either hands off to $speckit-upgrade OR offers to add a new integration alongside via `specify integration install <key>` — both are acceptable; the operator chooses

evals.prompt: Install speckit for codex only with skills mode in this empty repo.
evals.expected_output: Initializes the repo with `specify init --here --integration codex --integration-options="--skills"` so the Codex install uses the v0.8.13 skills-mode layout (.codex/skills/speckit-*/) rather than legacy slash commands.
expectations: Includes `--integration-options="--skills"` to opt into skills mode for Codex
Does not install Claude integration when the operator scoped to codex only
Confirms post-install that .codex/skills/speckit-*/ exists and not .codex/prompts/speckit.*.md
